mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 are negative in more than 40% of periods where RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4.0%, it signals an extremely tight labor market, which typically leads to wage inflation, compressing corporate profit margins and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:tight labor markets can lead to wage pressure
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE is below 4.0%, or if the average return in such periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A scenario where consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below the inflation rate indicates the central bank is behind the curve, suggesting future aggressive tightening that will negatively impact short-term equity valuations over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In an economic expansion regime, a consistent increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong investment and consumer confidence in the economy's future, driving increased construction activity and related spending, which positively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:housing as a leading economic indicator
regime_hint: economic expansion (e.g., positive GDPC1 growth, UNRATE below 5%)
failure_condition: If, during identified expansionary regimes, periods of rising HOUST do not correlate positively with 20-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1.0% signals a significant contraction in manufacturing and mining output, indicating weakening economic activity that will negatively impact corporate earnings and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1.0% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production as a coincident indicator
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods following an INDPRO decline greater than 1.0% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive monthly growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 are negative in more than 40% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is robust (e.g., > 2% annualized) while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is maintained at a relatively low level (e.g., < 2%), it indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting strong economic expansion, which is fundamentally bullish for equities over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: economic expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If the average 60-day S&P 500 forward return in triggered periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or if the Rank IC is below 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment, an unexpected or aggressive hike in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) by more than 0.25% in a single month signals a hawkish central bank stance, which can shock markets by increasing discount rates and lead to short-term equity declines over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25% in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:interest rate hikes impact equity valuations
regime_hint: high inflation environment (e.g., CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3%)
failure_condition: If, during identified high inflation regimes, periods following a FEDFUNDS hike > 0.25% in a month show positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.
